title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Detective Chief Inspector Cornish and Detective Constable Cornish interview Arthur Badcock regarding the death of his wife, Heather. The detectives inform him that the autopsy revealed Heather died from an overdose of Camon, a brand of drug used to treat anxiety and depression. Badcock denies that his wife required medication, asserting she was a happy person who had not seen her doctor, Dr. Sim, since moving to the area.

Badcock provides a detailed account of the charity gala held at the Goschington Estate. He explains that after having tea and cake in a courtyard tent, he and Heather were invited to a private reception on the upper floor of the house. There, they met the hostess, film star Marina Gregg, and her husband, Jason Radcliffe. Radcliffe served Heather a Daiquiri cocktail. While Heather was conversing with friends from the St. John Ambulance, an unidentified person touched her elbow, causing her drink to spill on both her and Miss Gregg. Following the spill, Miss Gregg handed Heather her own glass, claiming she had already had enough to drink. Heather drank from the glass, complained of feeling "off," and subsequently collapsed and died.

In a separate discussion, Chief Inspector Craddock and Mr. DeMortimer Clegg review the case. They note that Heather Badcock, the local secretary for the St. John Ambulance, had organized the gala. The event was hosted by Marina Gregg and Jason Radcliffe at Goschington Manor to support their new film company. The police consider the case significant due to the high-profile nature of the guests and the bizarre circumstances surrounding the poisoning.
